name_extraction: configEntryNameFromPath with prefix 'agent/' — strips directory prefix from filename

profile_validation.description: Orchestration controller for wave-model agent delegation
profile_validation.notes: Read/search/task-only, no mutation, no validation delegation.

profile_validation.description: Long-horizon senior engineer — adversarial review, claim falsification, architectural critique
profile_validation.notes: No bash at all (not even ask). Pure read-only review role. Stricter than 'read-only' baseline — no shell execution.

profile_validation.description: Narrow implementation worker — additive edits, dirty-file preservation, blast radius control, self-validation
profile_validation.notes: Full read/write access within mission boundary. Steps=60 (highest — most complex work). No checkpoint/publish.

profile_validation.description: Validation worker — schema validation, bounded checks, test/exercise surface, evidence integrity
profile_validation.notes: Read + bash for running validation commands. No mutation tools.

profile_validation.description: Hostile red-team agent — adversarial attack surface testing, 'break my newest mechanism'
profile_validation.notes: Read + bash for running stress commands. No mutation — 'you break, you do not fix'.

profile_validation.description: Narrow aftermath repair worker — JSON repair directives, delta-focused, single-seam targeting
profile_validation.notes: Same write access as executor but scoped to single-seam delta fixes from JSON repair directives.
Pre-existing profile — uses legacy `tools:` block (*: false, specific tool: true) rather than new `permission:` block. Both formats are valid and interoperable.
Pre-existing profile — uses legacy `tools:` block. Valid and interoperable.

Not a true agent profile — content is a permission doctrine reference document. Has mode: subagent and hidden: true, so it will be loaded by the agent glob as an agent definition. This is a minor impedance mismatch but functionally harmless (hidden agents are not user-selectable).

permission_chain_consistency.notes: All 6 Track 2 profiles consistently use `*: deny` as base permission, then selectively allow specific tools. The critic is the most restricted (no bash at all).

glob_compatibility.notes: All files use .md extension and live directly under .opencode/agent/, matching {agent,agents}/**/*.md. Names are correctly derived by stripping the 'agent/' prefix.

global_permission_config_consistency.notes: Pre-existing profiles override disabled global tools via their agent-level `tools:` blocks. This is the intended override pattern — agents enable tools the global config disables.

gaps.description: permissions.md is a reference document (permission doctrine) but has mode: subagent with hidden: true. The agent loading glob will load it as an agent definition. While functionally harmless (hidden agents are not user-visible), it is semantically misleading — a reference document should not be an agent profile.
gaps.recommendation: Either remove the mode/hidden frontmatter from permissions.md (making it a non-agent Markdown file that is simply ignored by the loader), or split into a true agent profile and a separate reference document.

gaps.description: Pre-existing profiles (duplicate-pr.md, triage.md) use the legacy `tools:` frontmatter block for permissions, while Track 2 profiles use the newer `permission:` block. Both formats are valid and interoperable at runtime, but the asymmetry may confuse maintenance.
gaps.recommendation: Consider migrating pre-existing profiles to the `permission:` format for consistency when they next need maintenance.
